text:
ByKasey Nelson,

Former Contributor.

Redemption tastes sweet for the U.S. women’s gymnastics team, winning the Olympic gold medal in the gymnastics team final inside of Bercy Arena in Paris on Tuesday. The veteran gymnasts on the squad had labeled this Olympic Games their “redemption tour” after winning silver in the Tokyo Games.  The U.S. scored 171.296 to win gold, while Italy took silver (165.494), their first Olympic team medal since 1928, and Brazil finished with the bronze (164.497).



Biles also made U.S. gymnastics history on Tuesday with her eighth Olympic medal, surpassing Shannon Miller. In addition to Biles, a three-time Olympian and Rio all-around champion, the U.S. team relied heavily on two-time Olympians Suni Lee (Tokyo all-around champion) and Jordan Chiles (Tokyo Team Silver) and Jade Carey (Tokyo floor champion). Biles and Chiles competed all-around, Suni Lee on bars, beam, and floor and Carey on vault.  Newcomer Hezly Rivera was not put in any of the four lineups.

For scoring in the women's gymnastics team final, each team puts up three athletes and every score counts toward the team total. The U.S. began on vault and rotated in Olympic order (vault, bars, beam and floor). Italy also started on vault, and Brazil and China began on bars.

First up was Chiles on vault, performing a Yurchenko double-full and earning a 14.4 to mark a great start for the U.S. team. Carey launched a Cheng vault (half onto the table front 1.5 twist off) with only a hop in place for a huge 14.8. While Biles decided against the Biles II due to calf issues, she competed the Cheng vault with a hop back on the landing for a 14.9 to lead the way for Team USA. The U.S. takes an early lead after rotation one, with China in second and Italy in third.

Onto rotation two, Chiles led off again for the U.S. on uneven bars, showing off her stunning lines and nearly sticking her full-twisting double back dismount (14.366). The crowd erupted as she shouted,"Let's go!" Biles also hit her bar set, dismounting with a double-double back for a 14.4. Last up for the U.S., Lee performed a huge Nabieva (toe on Tkatchev layout) to Bhardwaj (full-twisting pac salto) but hit her feet on the mat for a five-tenth deduction. She was able to stay on the equipment with no falls and finished with a full-twisting double back to still lead the way for Team USA with a 14.566.

The U.S. women increased their lead by 3.102 after falling on beam from both Brazil and China. China’s Zhou Yaqin suffered two falls, and Brazil’s Julia Soares also fell for a 12.4. Brazil's Rebeca Andrade felt the pressure with a big wobble but stayed on for a 14.133.

In the third rotation, Chiles began on beam with a fall on her front pike mount (one-point deduction) but finished the routine strong with a stuck double pike dismount. She scored a 12.733, which was not the beam start Team USA was looking for. The pressure was on Lee for a must-hit routine. She came up clutch for Team USA, hitting a stunning routine, including a side aerial to a layout, with no wobbles in sight. Lee fired a 14.6 to get the U.S. back on track. Biles exuded confidence and hit her beam set. She had a wobble on her side aerial but stayed on and dismounted with a big back handspring, back handspring two-foot to full-in dismount earning a 14.366 to put Team USA back on top. Italy finished the rotation in second while Great Britain surged to third.

The U.S. women headed to their fourth and final rotation with a commanding lead. Lee led off on floor with a stunning routine, including a double-double laid out, 1.5 twist to front full, and double back as the crowd went wild. She earned a 13.533. Chiles added energy into the arena with her Beyoncé-themed routine that had the crowd clapping to the music with a full-twisting double layout, front-full step out to full-in. She finished with a double layout and saluted in tears of joy, scoring a 13.966. Biles closed out for the U.S. with her Taylor Swift, “Are you ready for it?” themed routine. She showed her sky-high Biles II, front full thru to double-double, her signature Biles and double layout to clinch the gold for Team USA with a 14.666. Italy takes the silver medal and Brazil snags the bronze.
title: Simone Biles Leads U.S. Women's Gymnastics To Olympic Team Gold In Paris
author: Kasey Nelson
description: Redemption tastes sweet for the U.S. women’s gymnastics team, winning the Olympic gold medal in the gymnastics team final inside of Bercy Arena in Paris on Tuesday.
keywords: Simone Biles Olympics,Suni Lee Olympics,Jade Carey Olympics,Jordan Chiles Olympics,Hezly Rivera Olympics,USA Women's Gymnastics Team Final,2024 Paris Olympics Gymnastics Results,U.S. Women's Gymnastics Wins Gold,Italy Wins Silver,Brazil Wins Bronze
